{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: All referenced files exist at stated line numbers. The workerstatus package (scanner_linux.go, 6.9K) already provides /proc-based process scanning with LiveWorker structs, and orphan_harness_reaper.go establishes the injected-fake pattern for process census testing. TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup exists in attempt_backend_unix_test.go. The prior attempt was a transport failure (signal: terminated, outcome_reason=transport) with no implementation work completed — not a design rejection. The AC is numbered with specific test function names, platform-isolation is specified (build-tagged no-op), and the cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test gates are explicit. Implementation path: add a process-census method to ExecutionCleanupManager reusing workerstatus.Scanner, extend ExecutionCleanupSummary with process fields, update cleanupCommandReport, wire into work startup pre-claim, add platform-specific files following scanner_linux.go/scanner_other.go pattern.","fingerprint":"f72fb80d744dfa6467b2f8bcdc8d289a513cdfcf48fb6afd93f26f8b0832aa0a","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"f72fb80d744dfa6467b2f8bcdc8d289a513cdfcf48fb6afd93f26f8b0832aa0a"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: All five referenced source files exist with valid line ranges. Spot-checks confirm the described gaps: ExecutionCleanupSummary (execution_cleanup.go:110-138) has file/resource counts only and no process fields; orphan_harness_reaper (orphan_harness_reaper.go:75-105) filters only PPID=1 children with harness basenames; startup_housekeeping.go scanWorkerDirs removes stale dirs but never terminates or reports the underlying process tree. AC names specific test functions, includes a focused go test filter, and ends with a lefthook gate. Out-of-scope section cites five bead IDs with precise scope boundaries. Prior attempt 1 timed out at standard power class (result_rev == base_rev confirms no code was landed); attempt 2 is a transport failure unrelated to implementation complexity. Difficulty is hard: the timeout is direct evidence that standard dispatch was insufficient.","fingerprint":"8366f4100da5e695f0ebc14b283983f8853b074b6bc4a5f6d849286abb646471","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"timeout","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.timeout","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"8366f4100da5e695f0ebc14b283983f8853b074b6bc4a5f6d849286abb646471"}

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_DryRunReportsStaleAttemptDescendantProcesses` (or equivalent) proving a stale process whose cwd/worktree is under the current project's DDx execution root is reported with pid, command, worktree, stale reason, and `would_kill=true`, while dry-run does not call the killer.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes` AC1."},{"parent_ac":"2. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyReapsOnlyStaleAttemptProcessGroups` (or equivalent, using injected scanner/killer fakes where needed) proving `--apply` terminates only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups and preserves live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, and other-project processes.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: reap only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups` AC1 and AC2."},{"parent_ac":"3. Add `TestCleanupCommand_JSONIncludesProcessFindings` and a text-output assertion proving `ddx cleanup --json` and dry-run text include process findings/counts without breaking the existing stale temp dir/run-state/scratch report fields.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: expose stale process findings in ddx cleanup output` AC1, AC2, and AC3."},{"parent_ac":"4. Add `TestWorkStartupCleanup_ReapsStaleAttemptDescendantProcessesBeforeClaim` (or equivalent) proving work startup/pre-claim cleanup runs the same conservative process pass before claiming the next bead and emits a structured cleanup observation when it reaps or preserves a process group.","coverage":"Child `workers: run stale attempt process cleanup before bead claim` AC1 and AC2."},{"parent_ac":"5. Add a regression case for a non-harness descendant command such as `sh`, `cargo`, or a tiny long-lived test server under a stale execution worktree; it must be detected by DDx ownership/cwd/liveness evidence, not by broad command-name matching.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes` AC2, with safety preservation reinforced by child `cleanup: reap only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups` AC2."},{"parent_ac":"6. Unsupported platforms have a deterministic no-op/report-unavailable path covered by a build-tagged test or equivalent assertion; Linux behavior is covered by the process-census/reaper tests.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes` AC3 covers unsupported-platform no-op/report-unavailable behavior; Linux behavior is covered by child `cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes` AC1 and AC2 plus child `cleanup: reap only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups` AC1 and AC2."},{"parent_ac":"7. Existing process-reaper tests remain green: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes` AC4 and AC5; child `cleanup: reap only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups` AC4."},{"parent_ac":"8. Focused cleanup tests pass: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'Cleanup.*Process|AttemptDescendant|WorkStartupCleanup|CleanupCommand' -count=1` passes.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: expose stale process findings in ddx cleanup output` AC4 and child `workers: run stale attempt process cleanup before bead claim` AC4."},{"parent_ac":"9. Package-level gate passes: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: expose stale process findings in ddx cleanup output` AC5 and child `workers: run stale attempt process cleanup before bead claim` AC5."},{"parent_ac":"10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","coverage":"Child `cleanup: report stale attempt descendant processes` AC6; child `cleanup: reap only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups` AC6; child `cleanup: expose stale process findings in ddx cleanup output` AC6; child `workers: run stale attempt process cleanup before bead claim` AC6."}],"child_ids":["ddx-47d9100a","ddx-0f29e87a","ddx-76647136","ddx-6ee2b0c3"],"rationale":"The parent bead combines four implementation surfaces that can be executed and reviewed separately: process census/reporting in the cleanup manager, safe apply-mode process-group reaping, command text/JSON operator output, and work startup/pre-claim wiring. Each child preserves the parent non-scope constraints, parent/dependency context, FEAT-010 and SD-025 references, and has named Test* symbols plus focused go test and lefthook gates."}
